sec.compound: amino acids (glutamic acid, histidine, methionine, proline, phenylalanine)

growth_records.cite: Weiss, Anna S. et al. In vitro interaction network of a synthetic gut bacterial community. The ISME Journal (2022). DOI:10.1038/s41396-021-01153-z. [PMC8941000]
growth_records.snippet: strains with intermediate growth rate (GR > 1 h-1, M. intestinale YL27 ...) ... Both Bacteroidales strains produced acetic acid, succinic acid as well as branched-chain fatty acids ... In SM of strains M. intestinale YL27 and B. caecimuris
growth_records.notes: growth_rate 1.0 h-1 = lower bound of 'intermediate' bin (GR>1 h-1; exact in Table S2, unavailable). Bacteroidales fermentation: acetate + succinate + BCFA (qualitative, SCFA LC-MS). Net release of several amino acids into spent medium. Depletes >600 features (broadest substrate use). No numeric rates.

growth_records.cite: Pau Pérez Escriva et al. Distinct N and C Cross-Feeding Networks in a Synthetic Mouse Gut Consortium. mSystems (2022). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01484-21. [PMC9040589]
growth_records.snippet: Table 1: Muribaculum intestinale YL27 (Bacteroidetes), Max OD 0.93, Mean μ ± SD 0.39 ± 0.03. '...M. intestinale grew substantially slower.'
growth_records.notes: μ=0.39 h^-1 (±0.03) reported — slowest of the growers except A. muciniphila. td = ln2/μ = 0.6931/0.39 = 1.777 h. Max OD600 = 0.93. Named as primary carbon and nitrogen supplier in the consortium food webs; no per-biomass rates reported.
